auth_notes: Most modern Quality Management Systems (like Ideagen, MasterControl, or ETQ) utilize Bearer Token authentication. On-premise installations may require NTLM or Basic Auth via a VPN or local tunnel.
rate_limits: Typically 60-120 requests per minute depending on the specific vendor and license tier.
gotchas: Document uploads often require complex multipart/form-data handling in the HTTP Request node.
Pagination logic is frequently non-standard (e.g., using custom headers for 'next-page' links).
Strict schema validation for custom fields can cause 400 errors if types are not explicitly cast.
client_must_provide: Module-specific permissions (e.g., Document Control, CAPA, Audit)
operations_available: Create CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) Entry
research_notes: Since no native node exists, the integration must use the HTTP Request node. For document management, the 'Binary Data' property in n8n will be essential. If the QMS is on-premise, an n8n self-hosted instance or a secure tunnel (like Ngrok or Cloudflare) will be required to receive webhooks.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Since no native node exists, the integration must use the HTTP Request node. For document management, the 'Binary Data' property in n8n will be essential. If the QMS is on-premise, an n8n self-hosted instance or a secure tunnel (like Ngrok or Cloudflare) will be required to receive webhooks.
